tEn: Deep Reinforcement Learning with Double Q-learning
tZh: 深度强化学习中的双Q学习
sEn: Double Q-learning reduces overestimations in DQN, improving performance on Atari games.
sZh: 双Q学习减少了DQN中的过高估计，从而在多个Atari游戏中提升了性能。
absEn: The popular Q-learning algorithm is known to overestimate action values under certain conditions. It was not previously known whether, in practice, such overestimations are common, whether they harm performance, and whether they can generally be prevented. In this paper, we answer all these questions affirmatively. In particular, we first show that the recent DQN algorithm, which combines Q-learning with a deep neural network, suffers from substantial overestimations in some games in the Atari 2600 domain. We then show that the idea behind the Double Q-learning algorithm, which was introduced in a tabular setting, can be generalized to work with large-scale function approximation. We propose a specific adaptation to the DQN algorithm and show that the resulting algorithm not only reduces the observed overestimations, as hypothesized, but that this also leads to much better performance on several games.
absZh: 流行的Q学习算法在某些条件下会高估动作值。此前尚不清楚在实践中这种高估是否常见、是否损害性能，以及是否通常可以避免。在本文中，我们对所有这些问题给出了肯定答案。具体来说，我们首先展示了最近的DQN算法（将Q学习与深度神经网络相结合）在Atari 2600领域的某些游戏中存在严重的高估问题。然后，我们展示了在表格设置中引入的双Q学习算法的思想可以推广到大规模函数逼近。我们提出了对DQN算法的具体改编，并表明所得算法不仅如假设那样减少了观察到的高估，而且在多个游戏上带来了更好的性能。

contrib.en: Shows Q-learning overestimates action values in large-scale deterministic problems due to inherent estimation errors.
contrib.zh: 证明Q-learning在大规模确定性问题中因固有估计误差而高估动作值。

contrib.en: Demonstrates DQN suffers substantial overestimations in Atari 2600 games, harming performance.
contrib.zh: 展示DQN在Atari 2600游戏中存在严重高估，损害性能。

contrib.en: Generalizes Double Q-learning to large-scale function approximation with deep neural networks.
contrib.zh: 将Double Q-learning推广到深度神经网络的大规模函数逼近。

contrib.en: Proposes Double DQN algorithm, reducing overestimations and improving policy quality.
contrib.zh: 提出Double DQN算法，减少高估并提升策略质量。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art results on Atari 2600 domain with Double DQN.
contrib.zh: Double DQN在Atari 2600领域取得最先进结果。

limits.en: Evaluation limited to deterministic Atari 2600 games; stochastic environments not tested.
limits.zh: 评估仅限于确定性Atari 2600游戏，未测试随机环境。

limits.en: Hyperparameters tuned for DQN, not optimized for Double DQN in main comparison.
limits.zh: 超参数为DQN调优，主要比较中未针对Double DQN优化。

limits.en: Double DQN may not eliminate overestimations entirely; residual bias possible.
limits.zh: Double DQN可能无法完全消除高估，残留偏差仍可能存在。

limits.en: Theoretical analysis assumes unbiased estimates; practical violations may affect guarantees.
limits.zh: 理论分析假设无偏估计，实际偏差可能影响保证。

limits.en: Results may not generalize to other domains beyond Atari 2600.
limits.zh: 结果可能无法推广到Atari 2600以外的领域。

items.en: The popular Q-learning algorithm is known to overestimate action values under certain conditions. It was not previously known whether, in practice, such overestimations are common, whether they harm performance, and whether they can generally be prevented. In this paper, we answer all these questions affirmatively. In particular, we first show that the recent DQN algorithm, which combines Q-learning with a deep neural network, suffers from substantial overestimations in some games in the Atari 2600 domain. We then show that the idea behind the Double Q-learning algorithm, which was introduced in a tabular setting, can be generalized to work with large-scale function approximation. We propose a specific adaptation to the DQN algorithm and show that the resulting algorithm not only reduces the observed overestimations, as hypothesized, but that this also leads to much better performance on several games.
items.zh: 众所周知，流行的 Q-learning 算法在某些条件下会高估动作值。此前尚不清楚在实践中这种高估是否常见、是否损害性能，以及是否通常可以避免。在本文中，我们肯定地回答了所有这些问题。具体来说，我们首先表明，最近的 DQN 算法（将 Q-learning 与深度神经网络相结合）在 Atari 2600 领域的某些游戏中遭受了严重的高估。然后，我们展示了在表格设置中引入的 Double Q-learning 算法背后的思想可以推广到大规模函数逼近。我们提出了对 DQN 算法的具体改编，并表明所得算法不仅如假设那样减少了观察到的高估，而且在多个游戏上带来了更好的性能。

items.en: The goal of reinforcement learning (Sutton and Barto, 1998) is to learn good policies for sequential decision problems, by optimizing a cumulative future reward signal. Q-learning (Watkins, 1989) is one of the most popular reinforcement learning algorithms, but it is known to sometimes learn unrealistically high action values because it includes a maximization step over estimated action values, which tends to prefer overestimated to underestimated values.
items.zh: 强化学习（Sutton 和 Barto, 1998）的目标是通过优化累积的未来奖励信号，为顺序决策问题学习良好的策略。Q-learning（Watkins, 1989）是最流行的强化学习算法之一，但已知它有时会学习不切实际的高动作值，因为它包含对估计动作值的最大化步骤，这倾向于偏好高估值而非低估值。

items.en: In previous work, overestimations have been attributed to insufficiently flexible function approximation (Thrun and Schwartz, 1993) and noise (van Hasselt, 2010, 2011). In this paper, we unify these views and show overestimations can occur when the action values are inaccurate, irrespective of the source of approximation error. Of course, imprecise value estimates are the norm during learning, which indicates that overestimations may be much more common than previously appreciated.
items.zh: 在先前的工作中，高估被归因于函数逼近不够灵活（Thrun 和 Schwartz, 1993）和噪声（van Hasselt, 2010, 2011）。在本文中，我们统一了这些观点，并表明当动作值不准确时，无论近似误差的来源如何，都可能发生高估。当然，在学习过程中不精确的值估计是常态，这表明高估可能比之前认为的更为普遍。

items.en: It is an open question whether, if the overestimations do occur, this negatively affects performance in practice. Overoptimistic value estimates are not necessarily a problem in and of themselves. If all values would be uniformly higher then the relative action preferences are preserved and we would not expect the resulting policy to be any worse. Furthermore, it is known that sometimes it is good to be optimistic: optimism in the face of uncertainty is a well-known exploration technique (Kaelbling et al., 1996). If, however, the overestimations are not uniform and not concentrated at states about which we wish to learn more, then they might negatively affect the quality of the resulting policy. Thrun and Schwartz (1993) give specific examples in which this leads to suboptimal policies, even asymptotically.
items.zh: 一个悬而未决的问题是，如果确实发生高估，这在实践中是否会负面影响性能。过度乐观的值估计本身不一定是问题。如果所有值都统一更高，那么相对动作偏好得以保留，我们预计所得策略不会更差。此外，已知有时乐观是好的：面对不确定性时的乐观是一种众所周知的探索技术（Kaelbling 等人, 1996）。然而，如果高估不是均匀的，并且不集中在我们希望了解更多信息的状态上，那么它们可能会对所得策略的质量产生负面影响。Thrun 和 Schwartz（1993）给出了具体例子，表明即使渐近地，这也会导致次优策略。

items.en: To test whether overestimations occur in practice and at scale, we investigate the performance of the recent DQN algorithm (Mnih et al., 2015). DQN combines Q-learning with a flexible deep neural network and was tested on a varied and large set of deterministic Atari 2600 games, reaching human-level performance on many games. In some ways, this setting is a best-case scenario for Q-learning, because the deep neural network provides flexible function approximation with the potential for a low asymptotic approximation error, and the determinism of the environments prevents the harmful effects of noise. Perhaps surprisingly, we show that even in this comparatively favorable setting DQN sometimes substantially overestimates the values of the actions.
items.zh: 为了测试高估是否在实践中和大规模下发生，我们研究了最近的 DQN 算法（Mnih 等人, 2015）的性能。DQN 将 Q-learning 与灵活的深度神经网络相结合，并在大量多样化的确定性 Atari 2600 游戏上进行了测试，在许多游戏上达到了人类水平的表现。在某些方面，这种设置是 Q-learning 的最佳情况，因为深度神经网络提供了灵活的函数逼近，具有低渐近近似误差的潜力，并且环境的确定性防止了噪声的有害影响。也许令人惊讶的是，我们表明即使在这种相对有利的设置下，DQN 有时也会严重高估动作的值。

items.en: We show that the idea behind the Double Q-learning algorithm (van Hasselt, 2010), which was first proposed in a tabular setting, can be generalized to work with arbitrary function approximation, including deep neural networks. We use this to construct a new algorithm we call Double DQN. We then show that this algorithm not only yields more accurate value estimates, but leads to much higher scores on several games. This demonstrates that the overestimations of DQN were indeed leading to poorer policies and that it is beneficial to reduce them. In addition, by improving upon DQN we obtain state-of-the-art results on the Atari domain.
items.zh: 我们展示了 Double Q-learning 算法（van Hasselt, 2010）背后的思想（最初在表格设置中提出）可以推广到任意函数逼近，包括深度神经网络。我们利用这一点构建了一种新算法，称为 Double DQN。然后，我们表明该算法不仅产生更准确的值估计，而且在多个游戏上带来了更高的分数。这证明了 DQN 的高估确实导致了较差的策略，并且减少高估是有益的。此外，通过改进 DQN，我们在 Atari 领域获得了最先进的结果。

items.en: To solve sequential decision problems we can learn estimates for the optimal value of each action, defined as the expected sum of future rewards when taking that action and following the optimal policy thereafter. Under a given policy π𝜋\pi, the true value of an action a𝑎a in a state s𝑠s is
items.zh: 为了解决顺序决策问题，我们可以学习每个动作的最优值的估计，定义为在采取该动作并随后遵循最优策略时未来奖励的期望和。在给定策略 π 下，状态 s 中动作 a 的真实值为

items.en: where γ∈[0,1]𝛾01\gamma\in[0,1] is a discount factor that trades off the importance of immediate and later rewards. The optimal value is then Q∗​(s,a)=maxπ⁡Qπ​(s,a)subscript𝑄𝑠𝑎subscript𝜋subscript𝑄𝜋𝑠𝑎Q_{*}(s,a)=\max_{\pi}Q_{\pi}(s,a). An optimal policy is easily derived from the optimal values by selecting the highest-valued action in each state.
items.zh: 其中 γ∈[0,1] 是折扣因子，权衡即时奖励和后续奖励的重要性。最优值则为 Q*(s,a)=maxπ Qπ(s,a)。通过在每个状态中选择最高值的动作，可以轻松地从最优值推导出最优策略。

items.en: Estimates for the optimal action values can be learned using Q-learning (Watkins, 1989), a form of temporal difference learning (Sutton, 1988). Most interesting problems are too large to learn all action values in all states separately. Instead, we can learn a parameterized value function Q​(s,a;𝜽t)𝑄𝑠𝑎subscript𝜽𝑡Q(s,a;\bm{\theta}_{t}). The standard Q-learning update for the parameters after taking action Atsubscript𝐴𝑡A_{t} in state Stsubscript𝑆𝑡S_{t} and observing the immediate reward Rt+1subscript𝑅𝑡1R_{t+1} and resulting state St+1subscript𝑆𝑡1S_{t+1} is then
items.zh: 可以使用 Q-learning（Watkins, 1989）学习最优动作值的估计，这是一种时序差分学习（Sutton, 1988）的形式。大多数有趣的问题太大，无法单独学习所有状态下的所有动作值。相反，我们可以学习参数化的值函数 Q(s,a;θt)。在状态 St 中采取动作 At 并观察到即时奖励 Rt+1 和结果状态 St+1 后，参数的标准 Q-learning 更新为

items.en: where α𝛼\alpha is a scalar step size and the target YtQsubscriptsuperscript𝑌Q𝑡Y^{\text{Q}}_{t} is defined as
items.zh: 其中 α 是标量步长，目标 YtQ 定义为

items.en: This update resembles stochastic gradient descent, updating the current value Q​(St,At;𝜽t)𝑄subscript𝑆𝑡subscript𝐴𝑡subscript𝜽𝑡Q(S_{t},A_{t};\bm{\theta}_{t}) towards a target value YtQsubscriptsuperscript𝑌Q𝑡Y^{\text{Q}}_{t}.
items.zh: 此更新类似于随机梯度下降，将当前值 Q(St,At;θt) 更新为目标值 YtQ。

items.en: A deep Q network (DQN) is a multi-layered neural network that for a given state s𝑠s outputs a vector of action values Q​(s,⋅;𝜽)𝑄𝑠⋅𝜽Q(s,\cdot\,;\bm{\theta}), where 𝜽𝜽\bm{\theta} are the parameters of the network. For an n𝑛n-dimensional state space and an action space containing m𝑚m actions, the neural network is a function from ℝnsuperscriptℝ𝑛\mathbb{R}^{n} to ℝmsuperscriptℝ𝑚\mathbb{R}^{m}. Two important ingredients of the DQN algorithm as proposed by Mnih et al. (2015) are the use of a target network, and the use of experience replay. The target network, with parameters 𝜽−superscript𝜽\bm{\theta}^{-}, is the same as the online network except that its parameters are copied every τ𝜏\tau steps from the online network, so that then 𝜽t−=𝜽tsubscriptsuperscript𝜽𝑡subscript𝜽𝑡\bm{\theta}^{-}_{t}=\bm{\theta}_{t}, and kept fixed on all other steps. The target used by DQN is then
items.zh: 深度 Q 网络（DQN）是一个多层神经网络，对于给定状态 s 输出动作值向量 Q(s,·;θ)，其中 θ 是网络的参数。对于 n 维状态空间和包含 m 个动作的动作空间，神经网络是从 Rn 到 Rm 的函数。Mnih 等人（2015）提出的 DQN 算法的两个重要组成部分是使用目标网络和使用经验回放。目标网络（参数为 θ-）与在线网络相同，只是其参数每 τ 步从在线网络复制一次，因此 θt-=θt，并在其他所有步骤上保持固定。DQN 使用的目标为

items.en: For the experience replay (Lin, 1992), observed transitions are stored for some time and sampled uniformly from this memory bank to update the network. Both the target network and the experience replay dramatically improve the performance of the algorithm (Mnih et al., 2015).
items.zh: 对于经验回放（Lin, 1992），观察到的转换被存储一段时间，并从该记忆库中均匀采样以更新网络。目标网络和经验回放都显著提高了算法的性能（Mnih 等人, 2015）。

items.en: The max operator in standard Q-learning and DQN, in (2) and (3), uses the same values both to select and to evaluate an action. This makes it more likely to select overestimated values, resulting in overoptimistic value estimates. To prevent this, we can decouple the selection from the evaluation. This is the idea behind Double Q-learning (van Hasselt, 2010).
items.zh: 标准 Q-learning 和 DQN 中的 max 运算符在（2）和（3）中使用相同的值来选择并评估动作。这使得更有可能选择高估值，导致过度乐观的值估计。为了防止这种情况，我们可以将选择与评估解耦。这就是 Double Q-learning（van Hasselt, 2010）背后的思想。

items.en: In the original Double Q-learning algorithm, two value functions are learned by assigning each experience randomly to update one of the two value functions, such that there are two sets of weights, 𝜽𝜽\bm{\theta} and 𝜽′superscript𝜽′\bm{\theta}^{\prime}. For each update, one set of weights is used to determine the greedy policy and the other to determine its value. For a clear comparison, we can first untangle the selection and evaluation in Q-learning and rewrite its target (2) as
items.zh: 在原始的 Double Q-learning 算法中，通过将每个经验随机分配给两个值函数之一来学习两个值函数，因此有两组权重 θ 和 θ'。对于每次更新，一组权重用于确定贪婪策略，另一组用于确定其值。为了清晰比较，我们可以先解开 Q-learning 中的选择和评估，并将其目标（2）重写为

items.en: The Double Q-learning error can then be written as
items.zh: Double Q-learning 误差则可以写为

items.en: Notice that the selection of the action, in the arg⁡max\operatorname*{\arg\!\max}, is still due to the online weights 𝜽tsubscript𝜽𝑡\bm{\theta}_{t}. This means that, as in Q-learning, we are still estimating the value of the greedy policy according to the current values, as defined by 𝜽tsubscript𝜽𝑡\bm{\theta}_{t}. However, we use the second set of weights 𝜽t′superscriptsubscript𝜽𝑡′\bm{\theta}_{t}^{\prime} to fairly evaluate the value of this policy. This second set of weights can be updated symmetrically by switching the roles of 𝜽𝜽\bm{\theta} and 𝜽′superscript𝜽′\bm{\theta}^{\prime}.
items.zh: 注意，动作的选择（在 argmax 中）仍然由在线权重 θt 决定。这意味着，与 Q-learning 一样，我们仍然根据当前值（由 θt 定义）估计贪婪策略的值。然而，我们使用第二组权重 θt' 来公平地评估该策略的值。这第二组权重可以通过交换 θ 和 θ' 的角色来对称更新。

full.secZh: 估计误差导致的过度乐观

items.en: Q-learning’s overestimations were first investigated by Thrun and Schwartz (1993), who showed that if the action values contain random errors uniformly distributed in an interval [−ϵ,ϵ]italic-ϵitalic-ϵ[-\epsilon,\epsilon] then each target is overestimated up to γ​ϵ​m−1m+1𝛾italic-ϵ𝑚1𝑚1\gamma\epsilon\frac{m-1}{m+1}, where m𝑚m is the number of actions. In addition, Thrun and Schwartz give a concrete example in which these overestimations even asymptotically lead to sub-optimal policies, and show the overestimations manifest themselves in a small toy problem when using function approximation. Later van Hasselt (2010) argued that noise in the environment can lead to overestimations even when using tabular representation, and proposed Double Q-learning as a solution.
items.zh: Q-learning 的高估首先由 Thrun 和 Schwartz（1993）研究，他们表明如果动作值包含均匀分布在区间 [-ϵ,ϵ] 中的随机误差，则每个目标被高估高达 γϵ(m-1)/(m+1)，其中 m 是动作数量。此外，Thrun 和 Schwartz 给出了一个具体例子，其中这些高估甚至渐近地导致次优策略，并表明在使用函数逼近时，高估在一个小玩具问题中显现出来。后来 van Hasselt（2010）认为，即使使用表格表示，环境中的噪声也可能导致高估，并提出了 Double Q-learning 作为解决方案。

items.en: In this section we demonstrate more generally that estimation errors of any kind can induce an upward bias, regardless of whether these errors are due to environmental noise, function approximation, non-stationarity, or any other source. This is important, because in practice any method will incur some inaccuracies during learning, simply due to the fact that the true values are initially unknown.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们更一般地证明，任何类型的估计误差都可能引起向上偏差，无论这些误差是由于环境噪声、函数逼近、非平稳性还是任何其他来源。这很重要，因为在实践中，任何方法在学习过程中都会产生一些不准确性，仅仅因为真实值最初是未知的。

items.en: The result by Thrun and Schwartz (1993) cited above gives an upper bound to the overestimation for a specific setup, but it is also possible, and potentially more interesting, to derive a lower bound.
items.zh: 上面引用的 Thrun 和 Schwartz（1993）的结果给出了特定设置下高估的上界，但也可以（可能更有趣）推导出下界。

items.en: Consider a state s𝑠s in which all the true optimal action values are equal at Q∗​(s,a)=V∗​(s)subscript𝑄𝑠𝑎subscript𝑉𝑠Q_{*}(s,a)=V_{*}(s) for some V∗​(s)subscript𝑉𝑠V_{*}(s). Let Qtsubscript𝑄𝑡Q_{t} be arbitrary value estimates that are on the whole unbiased in the sense that ∑a(Qt​(s,a)−V∗​(s))=0subscript𝑎subscript𝑄𝑡𝑠𝑎subscript𝑉𝑠0\sum_{a}(Q_{t}(s,a)-V_{*}(s))=0, but that are not all correct, such that 1m​∑a(Qt​(s,a)−V∗​(s))2=C1𝑚subscript𝑎superscriptsubscript𝑄𝑡𝑠𝑎subscript𝑉𝑠2𝐶\frac{1}{m}\sum_{a}(Q_{t}(s,a)-V_{*}(s))^{2}=C for some C>0𝐶0C>0, where m≥2𝑚2m\geq 2 is the number of actions in s𝑠s. Under these conditions, maxa⁡Qt​(s,a)≥V∗​(s)+Cm−1subscript𝑎subscript𝑄𝑡𝑠𝑎subscript𝑉𝑠𝐶𝑚1\max_{a}Q_{t}(s,a)\geq V_{*}(s)+\sqrt{\frac{C}{m-1}}. This lower bound is tight. Under the same conditions, the lower bound on the absolute error of the Double Q-learning estimate is zero. (Proof in appendix.)
items.zh: 考虑一个状态 s，其中所有真实最优动作值都相等，即 Q*(s,a)=V*(s)，其中 V*(s) 为某个值。设 Qt 为任意值估计，整体上无偏，即 ∑_a (Qt(s,a)-V*(s))=0，但并非全部正确，使得 1/m ∑_a (Qt(s,a)-V*(s))^2 = C，其中 C>0，m≥2 是 s 中的动作数。在这些条件下，max_a Qt(s,a) ≥ V*(s) + √(C/(m-1))。该下界是紧的。在相同条件下，Double Q-learning 估计的绝对误差下界为零。（证明见附录）

items.en: Note that we did not need to assume that estimation errors for different actions are independent. This theorem shows that even if the value estimates are on average correct, estimation errors of any source can drive the estimates up and away from the true optimal values.
items.zh: 注意，我们不需要假设不同动作的估计误差是独立的。该定理表明，即使值估计平均正确，任何来源的估计误差都可能使估计值偏离真实最优值。

items.en: The lower bound in Theorem 1 decreases with the number of actions. This is an artifact of considering the lower bound, which requires very specific values to be attained. More typically, the overoptimism increases with the number of actions as shown in Figure 1.
items.zh: 定理 1 中的下界随动作数增加而减小。这是考虑下界的一个假象，因为达到该下界需要非常特定的值。更典型的情况是，过度乐观随动作数增加而增加，如图 1 所示。

items.capEn: Figure 1: The orange bars show the bias in a single Q-learning update when the action values are Q​(s,a)=V∗​(s)+ϵa𝑄𝑠𝑎subscript𝑉𝑠subscriptitalic-ϵ𝑎Q(s,a)=V_{*}(s)+\epsilon_{a} and the errors {ϵa}a=1msuperscriptsubscriptsubscriptitalic-ϵ𝑎𝑎1𝑚\{\epsilon_{a}\}_{a=1}^{m} are independent standard normal random variables. The second set of action values Q′superscript𝑄′Q^{\prime}, used for the blue bars, was generated identically and independently. All bars are the average of 100 repetitions.
items.capZh: 图 1：橙色条显示单次 Q-learning 更新中的偏差，其中动作值为 Q(s,a)=V*(s)+ε_a，误差 {ε_a} 是独立的标准正态随机变量。用于蓝色条的第二组动作值 Q' 是独立同分布生成的。所有条是 100 次重复的平均值。

items.en: Q-learning’s overestimations there indeed increase with the number of actions, while Double Q-learning is unbiased. As another example, if for all actions Q∗​(s,a)=V∗​(s)subscript𝑄𝑠𝑎subscript𝑉𝑠Q_{*}(s,a)=V_{*}(s) and the estimation errors Qt​(s,a)−V∗​(s)subscript𝑄𝑡𝑠𝑎subscript𝑉𝑠Q_{t}(s,a)-V_{*}(s) are uniformly random in [−1,1]11[-1,1], then the overoptimism is m−1m+1𝑚1𝑚1\frac{m-1}{m+1}. (Proof in appendix.)
items.zh: Q-learning 的过高估计确实随动作数增加而增加，而 Double Q-learning 是无偏的。另一个例子，如果对于所有动作 Q*(s,a)=V*(s)，且估计误差 Qt(s,a)-V*(s) 在 [-1,1] 上均匀随机，则过度乐观为 (m-1)/(m+1)。（证明见附录）

items.capEn: Figure 2: Illustration of overestimations during learning. In each state (x-axis), there are 10 actions. The left column shows the true values V∗​(s)subscript𝑉𝑠V_{*}(s) (purple line). All true action values are defined by Q∗​(s,a)=V∗​(s)subscript𝑄𝑠𝑎subscript𝑉𝑠Q_{*}(s,a)=V_{*}(s). The green line shows estimated values Q​(s,a)𝑄𝑠𝑎Q(s,a) for one action as a function of state, fitted to the true value at several sampled states (green dots). The middle column plots show all the estimated values (green), and the maximum of these values (dashed black). The maximum is higher than the true value (purple, left plot) almost everywhere. The right column plots shows the difference in orange. The blue line in the right plots is the estimate used by Double Q-learning with a second set of samples for each state. The blue line is much closer to zero, indicating less bias. The three rows correspond to different true functions (left, purple) or capacities of the fitted function (left, green). (Details in the text)
items.capZh: 图 2：学习过程中过高估计的图示。在每个状态（x 轴）中，有 10 个动作。左列显示真实值 V*(s)（紫色线）。所有真实动作值定义为 Q*(s,a)=V*(s)。绿线显示一个动作的估计值 Q(s,a) 作为状态的函数，拟合于几个采样状态处的真实值（绿点）。中间列图显示所有估计值（绿色）以及这些值的最大值（黑色虚线）。最大值几乎处处高于真实值（紫色，左图）。右列图以橙色显示差值。右图中的蓝线是 Double Q-learning 使用的估计值，它使用每个状态的第二组样本。蓝线更接近零，表明偏差更小。三行对应不同的真实函数（左，紫色）或拟合函数的容量（左，绿色）。（细节见正文）

items.en: We now turn to function approximation and consider a real-valued continuous state space with 10 discrete actions in each state. For simplicity, the true optimal action values in this example depend only on state so that in each state all actions have the same true value. These true values are shown in the left column of plots in Figure 2 (purple lines) and are defined as either Q∗​(s,a)=sin⁡(s)subscript𝑄𝑠𝑎𝑠Q_{*}(s,a)=\sin(s) (top row) or Q∗​(s,a)=2​exp⁡(−s2)subscript𝑄𝑠𝑎2superscript𝑠2Q_{*}(s,a)=2\exp(-s^{2}) (middle and bottom rows). The left plots also show an approximation for a single action (green lines) as a function of state as well as the samples the estimate is based on (green dots). The estimate is a d𝑑d-degree polynomial that is fit to the true values at sampled states, where d=6𝑑6d=6 (top and middle rows) or d=9𝑑9d=9 (bottom row). The samples match the true function exactly: there is no noise and we assume we have ground truth for the action value on these sampled states. The approximation is inexact even on the sampled states for the top two rows because the function approximation is insufficiently flexible. In the bottom row, the function is flexible enough to fit the green dots, but this reduces the accuracy in unsampled states. Notice that the sampled states are spaced further apart near the left side of the left plots, resulting in larger estimation errors. In many ways this is a typical learning setting, where at each point in time we only have limited data.
items.zh: 现在我们转向函数逼近，考虑一个实值连续状态空间，每个状态有 10 个离散动作。为简单起见，本例中真实最优动作值仅依赖于状态，因此每个状态中所有动作具有相同的真实值。这些真实值如图 2 左列图所示（紫色线），定义为 Q*(s,a)=sin(s)（顶行）或 Q*(s,a)=2exp(-s^2)（中间和底行）。左图还显示单个动作的逼近（绿线）作为状态的函数，以及估计所基于的样本（绿点）。估计是 d 次多项式，在采样状态处拟合真实值，其中 d=6（顶行和中间行）或 d=9（底行）。样本与真实函数完全匹配：没有噪声，我们假设在这些采样状态上具有动作值的真实值。对于顶行和中间行，即使在采样状态上逼近也不精确，因为函数逼近不够灵活。在底行，函数足够灵活以拟合绿点，但这降低了未采样状态上的精度。注意，左图左侧的采样状态间隔较大，导致更大的估计误差。在许多方面，这是一个典型的学习设置，其中在每个时间点我们只有有限的数据。

items.en: The middle column of plots in Figure 2 shows estimated action value functions for all 10 actions (green lines), as functions of state, along with the maximum action value in each state (black dashed line). Although the true value function is the same for all actions, the approximations differ because we have supplied different sets of sampled states.111Each action-value function is fit with a different subset of integer states. States −66-6 and 666 are always included to avoid extrapolations, and for each action two adjacent integers are missing: for action a1subscript𝑎1a_{1} states −55-5 and −44-4 are not sampled, for a2subscript𝑎2a_{2} states −44-4 and −33-3 are not sampled, and so on. This causes the estimated values to differ. The maximum is often higher than the ground truth shown in purple on the left. This is confirmed in the right plots, which shows the difference between the black and purple curves in orange. The orange line is almost always positive, indicating an upward bias. The right plots also show the estimates from Double Q-learning in blue222We arbitrarily used the samples of action ai+5subscript𝑎𝑖5a_{i+5} (for i≤5𝑖5i\leq 5) or ai−5subscript𝑎𝑖5a_{i-5} (for i>5𝑖5i>5) as the second set of samples for the double estimator of action aisubscript𝑎𝑖a_{i}., which are on average much closer to zero. This demonstrates that Double Q-learning indeed can successfully reduce the overoptimism of Q-learning.
items.zh: 图 2 的中间列图显示了所有 10 个动作的估计动作值函数（绿线）作为状态的函数，以及每个状态中的最大动作值（黑色虚线）。尽管所有动作的真实值函数相同，但逼近不同，因为我们提供了不同的采样状态集。¹ 这导致估计值不同。最大值通常高于左侧紫色显示的真实值。这在右图中得到确认，右图以橙色显示黑色和紫色曲线之间的差值。橙色线几乎总是正的，表明向上偏差。右图还以蓝色显示 Double Q-learning 的估计值²，平均而言更接近零。这表明 Double Q-learning 确实能够成功减少 Q-learning 的过度乐观。

items.en: The different rows in Figure 2 show variations of the same experiment. The difference between the top and middle rows is the true value function, demonstrating that overestimations are not an artifact of a specific true value function. The difference between the middle and bottom rows is the flexibility of the function approximation. In the left-middle plot, the estimates are even incorrect for some of the sampled states because the function is insufficiently flexible. The function in the bottom-left plot is more flexible but this causes higher estimation errors for unseen states, resulting in higher overestimations. This is important because flexible parametric function approximators are often employed in reinforcement learning (see, e.g., Tesauro 1995; Sallans and Hinton 2004; Riedmiller 2005; Mnih et al. 2015).
items.zh: 图 2 中的不同行显示了同一实验的变体。顶行和中间行之间的差异在于真实值函数，表明过高估计不是特定真实值函数的假象。中间行和底行之间的差异在于函数逼近的灵活性。在左中图中，由于函数不够灵活，估计值甚至在一些采样状态上也不正确。左下图的函数更灵活，但这导致对未见状态更高的估计误差，从而产生更高的过高估计。这一点很重要，因为灵活的参数化函数逼近器经常用于强化学习（例如，Tesauro 1995；Sallans 和 Hinton 2004；Riedmiller 2005；Mnih 等人 2015）。

items.en: In contrast to van Hasselt (2010) we did not use a statistical argument to find overestimations, the process to obtain Figure 2 is fully deterministic. In contrast to Thrun and Schwartz (1993), we did not rely on inflexible function approximation with irreducible asymptotic errors; the bottom row shows that a function that is flexible enough to cover all samples leads to high overestimations. This indicates that the overestimations can occur quite generally.
items.zh: 与 van Hasselt (2010) 不同，我们没有使用统计论证来发现过高估计；获得图 2 的过程是完全确定性的。与 Thrun 和 Schwartz (1993) 不同，我们没有依赖具有不可约渐近误差的不灵活函数逼近；底行显示，足够灵活以覆盖所有样本的函数会导致高的过高估计。这表明过高估计可能相当普遍地发生。

items.en: In the examples above, overestimations occur even when assuming we have samples of the true action value at certain states. The value estimates can further deteriorate if we bootstrap off of action values that are already overoptimistic, since this causes overestimations to propagate throughout our estimates. Although uniformly overestimating values might not hurt the resulting policy, in practice overestimation errors will differ for different states and actions. Overestimation combined with bootstrapping then has the pernicious effect of propagating the wrong relative information about which states are more valuable than others, directly affecting the quality of the learned policies.
items.zh: 在上述例子中，即使假设我们在某些状态具有真实动作值的样本，也会发生过高估计。如果我们从已经过度乐观的动作值进行自举，值估计会进一步恶化，因为这会导致过高估计在我们的估计中传播。尽管统一高估值可能不会损害最终策略，但在实践中，过高估计误差在不同状态和动作上会不同。过高估计与自举相结合，会产生传播关于哪些状态比其他状态更有价值的错误相对信息的恶性影响，直接影响学习策略的质量。

items.en: The overestimations should not be confused with optimism in the face of uncertainty (Sutton, 1990; Agrawal, 1995; Kaelbling et al., 1996; Auer et al., 2002; Brafman and Tennenholtz, 2003; Szita and Lőrincz, 2008; Strehl et al., 2009), where an exploration bonus is given to states or actions with uncertain values. Conversely, the overestimations discussed here occur only after updating, resulting in overoptimism in the face of apparent certainty. This was already observed by Thrun and Schwartz (1993), who noted that, in contrast to optimism in the face of uncertainty, these overestimations actually can impede learning an optimal policy. We will see this negative effect on policy quality confirmed later in the experiments as well: when we reduce the overestimations using Double Q-learning, the policies improve.
items.zh: 过高估计不应与面对不确定性的乐观（Sutton, 1990; Agrawal, 1995; Kaelbling 等人, 1996; Auer 等人, 2002; Brafman 和 Tennenholtz, 2003; Szita 和 Lőrincz, 2008; Strehl 等人, 2009）混淆，后者对值不确定的状态或动作给予探索奖励。相反，这里讨论的过高估计仅在更新后发生，导致面对表面确定性时的过度乐观。Thrun 和 Schwartz (1993) 已经观察到这一点，他们指出，与面对不确定性的乐观相反，这些过高估计实际上会阻碍最优策略的学习。我们将在后面的实验中看到这种对策略质量的负面影响：当我们使用 Double Q-learning 减少过高估计时，策略得到改善。

items.en: The idea of Double Q-learning is to reduce overestimations by decomposing the max operation in the target into action selection and action evaluation. Although not fully decoupled, the target network in the DQN architecture provides a natural candidate for the second value function, without having to introduce additional networks. We therefore propose to evaluate the greedy policy according to the online network, but using the target network to estimate its value. In reference to both Double Q-learning and DQN, we refer to the resulting algorithm as Double DQN. Its update is the same as for DQN, but replacing the target YtDQNsubscriptsuperscript𝑌DQN𝑡Y^{\text{DQN}}_{t} with
items.zh: Double Q-learning 的思想是通过将目标中的 max 操作分解为动作选择和动作评估来减少过高估计。尽管没有完全解耦，DQN 架构中的目标网络为第二个值函数提供了一个自然候选，而无需引入额外的网络。因此，我们建议根据在线网络评估贪婪策略，但使用目标网络来估计其值。参考 Double Q-learning 和 DQN，我们将得到的算法称为 Double DQN。其更新与 DQN 相同，但将目标 Y_t^{DQN} 替换为

items.en: In comparison to Double Q-learning (4), the weights of the second network 𝜽t′subscriptsuperscript𝜽′𝑡\bm{\theta}^{\prime}_{t} are replaced with the weights of the target network 𝜽t−subscriptsuperscript𝜽𝑡\bm{\theta}^{-}_{t} for the evaluation of the current greedy policy. The update to the target network stays unchanged from DQN, and remains a periodic copy of the online network.
items.zh: 与 Double Q-learning (4) 相比，第二个网络 θ'_t 的权重被替换为目标网络 θ_t^- 的权重，用于评估当前贪婪策略。目标网络的更新与 DQN 保持不变，仍然是定期复制在线网络。

items.en: This version of Double DQN is perhaps the minimal possible change to DQN towards Double Q-learning. The goal is to get most of the benefit of Double Q-learning, while keeping the rest of the DQN algorithm intact for a fair comparison, and with minimal computational overhead.
items.zh: 这个版本的 Double DQN 可能是对 DQN 向 Double Q-learning 的最小可能改动。目标是在保持 DQN 算法其余部分不变以进行公平比较的同时，获得 Double Q-learning 的大部分好处，并且计算开销最小。

items.en: In this section, we analyze the overestimations of DQN and show that Double DQN improves over DQN both in terms of value accuracy and in terms of policy quality. To further test the robustness of the approach we additionally evaluate the algorithms with random starts generated from expert human trajectories, as proposed by Nair et al. (2015).
items.zh: 在本节中，我们分析 DQN 的过高估计，并展示 Double DQN 在值准确性和策略质量方面均优于 DQN。为了进一步测试方法的鲁棒性，我们还评估了从专家人类轨迹生成的随机起始点的算法，如 Nair 等人 (2015) 所提出的。

items.en: Our testbed consists of Atari 2600 games, using the Arcade Learning Environment (Bellemare et al., 2013). The goal is for a single algorithm, with a fixed set of hyperparameters, to learn to play each of the games separately from interaction given only the screen pixels as input. This is a demanding testbed: not only are the inputs high-dimensional, the game visuals and game mechanics vary substantially between games. Good solutions must therefore rely heavily on the learning algorithm — it is not practically feasible to overfit the domain by relying only on tuning.
items.zh: 我们的测试平台包括 Atari 2600 游戏，使用 Arcade Learning Environment (Bellemare 等人, 2013)。目标是使用单一算法，具有固定的超参数集，仅根据屏幕像素作为输入，通过交互分别学习玩每个游戏。这是一个要求很高的测试平台：不仅输入是高维的，游戏视觉和游戏机制在不同游戏之间也有很大差异。因此，好的解决方案必须严重依赖学习算法——仅通过调参来过度拟合领域实际上是不可行的。

items.en: We closely follow the experimental setting and network architecture outlined by Mnih et al. (2015). Briefly, the network architecture is a convolutional neural network (Fukushima, 1988; LeCun et al., 1998) with 3 convolution layers and a fully-connected hidden layer (approximately 1.5M parameters in total). The network takes the last four frames as input and outputs the action value of each action. On each game, the network is trained on a single GPU for 200M frames, or approximately 1 week.
items.zh: 我们紧密遵循 Mnih 等人 (2015) 概述的实验设置和网络架构。简而言之，网络架构是一个卷积神经网络 (Fukushima, 1988; LeCun 等人, 1998)，具有 3 个卷积层和一个全连接隐藏层（总共约 150 万个参数）。网络将最后四帧作为输入，并输出每个动作的动作值。在每个游戏上，网络在单个 GPU 上训练 2 亿帧，大约一周。

items.capEn: Figure 3: The top and middle rows show value estimates by DQN (orange) and Double DQN (blue) on six Atari games. The results are obtained by running DQN and Double DQN with 6 different random seeds with the hyper-parameters employed by Mnih et al. (2015). The darker line shows the median over seeds and we average the two extreme values to obtain the shaded area (i.e., 10% and 90% quantiles with linear interpolation). The straight horizontal orange (for DQN) and blue (for Double DQN) lines in the top row are computed by running the corresponding agents after learning concluded, and averaging the actual discounted return obtained from each visited state. These straight lines would match the learning curves at the right side of the plots if there is no bias. The middle row shows the value estimates (in log scale) for two games in which DQN’s overoptimism is quite extreme. The bottom row shows the detrimental effect of this on the score achieved by the agent as it is evaluated during training: the scores drop when the overestimations begin. Learning with Double DQN is much more stable.
items.capZh: 图 3：顶行和中间行显示 DQN（橙色）和 Double DQN（蓝色）在六个 Atari 游戏上的值估计。结果通过运行 DQN 和 Double DQN 使用 Mnih 等人 (2015) 采用的超参数和 6 个不同随机种子获得。较深的线显示种子间的中位数，我们平均两个极值以获得阴影区域（即，通过线性插值的 10% 和 90% 分位数）。顶行中的水平直线橙色（DQN）和蓝色（Double DQN）线通过在学习结束后运行相应智能体并平均从每个访问状态获得的实际折扣回报计算。如果无偏差，这些直线将与图右侧的学习曲线匹配。中间行以对数尺度显示两个游戏中 DQN 的过度乐观相当极端时的值估计。底行显示这对智能体在训练期间评估时获得的分数的不利影响：当过高估计开始时分数下降。使用 Double DQN 的学习更加稳定。

items.en: The ground truth averaged values are obtained by running the best learned policies for several episodes and computing the actual cumulative rewards. Without overestimations we would expect these quantities to match up (i.e., the curve to match the straight line at the right of each plot). Instead, the learning curves of DQN consistently end up much higher than the true values. The learning curves for Double DQN, shown in blue, are much closer to the blue straight line representing the true value of the final policy. Note that the blue straight line is often higher than the orange straight line. This indicates that Double DQN does not just produce more accurate value estimates but also better policies.
items.zh: 真实平均值的获得是通过运行最佳学习策略几个回合并计算实际累积奖励。如果没有过高估计，我们会期望这些量匹配（即曲线与每个图右侧的直线匹配）。相反，DQN 的学习曲线始终远高于真实值。Double DQN 的学习曲线（蓝色）更接近代表最终策略真实值的蓝色直线。注意，蓝色直线通常高于橙色直线。这表明 Double DQN 不仅产生更准确的值估计，而且产生更好的策略。

items.en: More extreme overestimations are shown in the middle two plots, where DQN is highly unstable on the games Asterix and Wizard of Wor. Notice the log scale for the values on the y𝑦y-axis. The bottom two plots shows the corresponding scores for these two games. Notice that the increases in value estimates for DQN in the middle plots coincide with decreasing scores in bottom plots. Again, this indicates that the overestimations are harming the quality of the resulting policies. If seen in isolation, one might perhaps be tempted to think the observed instability is related to inherent instability problems of off-policy learning with function approximation (Baird, 1995; Tsitsiklis and Van Roy, 1997; Sutton et al., 2008; Maei, 2011; Sutton et al., 2015). However, we see that learning is much more stable with Double DQN, suggesting that the cause for these instabilities is in fact Q-learning’s overoptimism. Figure 3 only shows a few examples, but overestimations were observed for DQN in all 49 tested Atari games, albeit in varying amounts.
items.zh: 中间两个图显示了更极端的过高估计，其中 DQN 在游戏 Asterix 和 Wizard of Wor 上高度不稳定。注意 y 轴上的值采用对数刻度。底部两个图显示了这两个游戏的相应得分。注意中间图中 DQN 的价值估计增加与底部图中得分下降同时发生。这再次表明过高估计损害了所得策略的质量。如果孤立地看，人们可能会认为观察到的这种不稳定与使用函数逼近的离策略学习固有的不稳定问题有关（Baird, 1995; Tsitsiklis and Van Roy, 1997; Sutton et al., 2008; Maei, 2011; Sutton et al., 2015）。然而，我们看到使用 Double DQN 时学习要稳定得多，这表明这些不稳定的原因实际上是 Q-learning 的过度乐观。图 3 只显示了几个例子，但在所有 49 个测试的 Atari 游戏中都观察到了 DQN 的过高估计，尽管程度不同。

items.en: Overoptimism does not always adversely affect the quality of the learned policy. For example, DQN achieves optimal behavior in Pong despite slightly overestimating the policy value. Nevertheless, reducing overestimations can significantly benefit the stability of learning; we see clear examples of this in Figure 3. We now assess more generally how much Double DQN helps in terms of policy quality by evaluating on all 49 games that DQN was tested on.
items.zh: 过度乐观并不总是对学习策略的质量产生不利影响。例如，DQN 在 Pong 中实现了最优行为，尽管略微高估了策略价值。然而，减少过高估计可以显著提高学习的稳定性；我们在图 3 中看到了清晰的例子。现在我们通过评估 DQN 测试过的所有 49 个游戏，更一般地评估 Double DQN 在策略质量方面的帮助程度。

items.en: As described by Mnih et al. (2015) each evaluation episode starts by executing a special no-op action that does not affect the environment up to 30 times, to provide different starting points for the agent. Some exploration during evaluation provides additional randomization. For Double DQN we used the exact same hyper-parameters as for DQN, to allow for a controlled experiment focused just on reducing overestimations. The learned policies are evaluated for 5 mins of emulator time (18,000 frames) with an ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon-greedy policy where ϵ=0.05italic-ϵ0.05\epsilon=0.05. The scores are averaged over 100 episodes. The only difference between Double DQN and DQN is the target, using YtDoubleDQNsubscriptsuperscript𝑌DoubleDQN𝑡Y^{\text{DoubleDQN}}_{t} rather than YDQNsuperscript𝑌DQNY^{\text{DQN}}. This evaluation is somewhat adversarial, as the used hyper-parameters were tuned for DQN but not for Double DQN.
items.zh: 如 Mnih 等人（2015）所述，每个评估回合开始时执行最多 30 次不影响环境的特殊无操作动作，以便为智能体提供不同的起始点。评估期间的某些探索提供了额外的随机化。对于 Double DQN，我们使用了与 DQN 完全相同的超参数，以便进行仅关注减少过高估计的受控实验。学习策略在 5 分钟的模拟器时间（18,000 帧）内进行评估，使用 ϵ=0.05 的 ϵ-贪婪策略。得分在 100 个回合上取平均。Double DQN 和 DQN 之间的唯一区别是目标，使用 Y_t^{DoubleDQN} 而不是 Y^{DQN}。这种评估有些不利，因为所使用的超参数是针对 DQN 调整的，而不是针对 Double DQN。

items.en: To obtain summary statistics across games, we normalize the score for each game as follows:
items.zh: 为了获得跨游戏的汇总统计，我们按如下方式对每个游戏的得分进行归一化：

items.en: The ‘random’ and ‘human’ scores are the same as used by Mnih et al. (2015), and are given in the appendix.
items.zh: “随机”和“人类”得分与 Mnih 等人（2015）使用的相同，并在附录中给出。

items.en: The Gorila algorithm (Nair et al., 2015), which is a massively distributed version of DQN, is not included in the table because the architecture and infrastructure is sufficiently different to make a direct comparison unclear. For completeness, we note that Gorila obtained median and mean normalized scores of 96% and 495%, respectively.
items.zh: Gorila 算法（Nair 等人，2015）是 DQN 的大规模分布式版本，未包含在表中，因为架构和基础设施差异较大，无法直接比较。为完整起见，我们注意到 Gorila 的中位数和平均归一化得分分别为 96% 和 495%。

full.secZh: 对人类起始状态的鲁棒性

items.en: One concern with the previous evaluation is that in deterministic games with a unique starting point the learner could potentially learn to remember sequences of actions without much need to generalize. While successful, the solution would not be particularly robust. By testing the agents from various starting points, we can test whether the found solutions generalize well, and as such provide a challenging testbed for the learned polices (Nair et al., 2015).
items.zh: 先前评估的一个担忧是，在具有唯一起始点的确定性游戏中，学习器可能学会记住动作序列，而无需太多泛化。虽然成功，但解决方案不会特别鲁棒。通过从各种起始点测试智能体，我们可以测试找到的解决方案是否泛化良好，从而为学习策略提供具有挑战性的测试平台（Nair 等人，2015）。

items.en: We obtained 100 starting points sampled for each game from a human expert’s trajectory, as proposed by Nair et al. (2015). We start an evaluation episode from each of these starting points and run the emulator for up to 108,000 frames (30 mins at 60Hz including the trajectory before the starting point). Each agent is only evaluated on the rewards accumulated after the starting point.
items.zh: 我们按照 Nair 等人（2015）的建议，从人类专家的轨迹中为每个游戏采样了 100 个起始点。我们从每个起始点开始一个评估回合，并运行模拟器最多 108,000 帧（60Hz 下 30 分钟，包括起始点之前的轨迹）。每个智能体仅根据起始点之后累积的奖励进行评估。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Normalized scores on 57 Atari games, tested for 100 episodes per game with human starts. Compared to Mnih et al. (2015), eight games additional games were tested. These are indicated with stars and a bold font.
items.capZh: 图 4：57 个 Atari 游戏的归一化得分，每个游戏使用人类起始点进行 100 个回合的测试。与 Mnih 等人（2015）相比，额外测试了八个游戏。这些游戏用星号和粗体表示。

items.en: For this evaluation we include a tuned version of Double DQN. Some tuning is appropriate because the hyperparameters were tuned for DQN, which is a different algorithm. For the tuned version of Double DQN, we increased the number of frames between each two copies of the target network from 10,000 to 30,000, to reduce overestimations further because immediately after each switch DQN and Double DQN both revert to Q-learning. In addition, we reduced the exploration during learning from ϵ=0.1italic-ϵ0.1\epsilon=0.1 to ϵ=0.01italic-ϵ0.01\epsilon=0.01, and then used ϵ=0.001italic-ϵ0.001\epsilon=0.001 during evaluation. Finally, the tuned version uses a single shared bias for all action values in the top layer of the network. Each of these changes improved performance and together they result in clearly better results.333Except for Tennis, where the lower ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon during training seemed to hurt rather than help.
items.zh: 对于此评估，我们包含了 Double DQN 的调优版本。进行一些调优是合适的，因为超参数是针对 DQN（一种不同的算法）调整的。对于 Double DQN 的调优版本，我们将目标网络每次复制之间的帧数从 10,000 增加到 30,000，以进一步减少过高估计，因为每次切换后 DQN 和 Double DQN 都会恢复到 Q-learning。此外，我们将学习期间的探索从 ϵ=0.1 减少到 ϵ=0.01，然后在评估中使用 ϵ=0.001。最后，调优版本在网络的顶层为所有动作值使用单个共享偏置。这些更改中的每一个都提高了性能，并且它们共同产生了明显更好的结果。³ 除了 Tennis，其中训练期间较低的 ϵ 似乎有害而不是有益。

items.en: Double DQN appears more robust to this more challenging evaluation, suggesting that appropriate generalizations occur and that the found solutions do not exploit the determinism of the environments. This is appealing, as it indicates progress towards finding general solutions rather than a deterministic sequence of steps that would be less robust.
items.zh: Double DQN 对这种更具挑战性的评估显得更加鲁棒，表明发生了适当的泛化，并且找到的解决方案没有利用环境的确定性。这是有吸引力的，因为它表明朝着找到通用解决方案而不是确定性步骤序列（后者鲁棒性较差）取得了进展。

items.en: This paper has five contributions. First, we have shown why Q-learning can be overoptimistic in large-scale problems, even if these are deterministic, due to the inherent estimation errors of learning. Second, by analyzing the value estimates on Atari games we have shown that these overestimations are more common and severe in practice than previously acknowledged. Third, we have shown that Double Q-learning can be used at scale to successfully reduce this overoptimism, resulting in more stable and reliable learning. Fourth, we have proposed a specific implementation called Double DQN, that uses the existing architecture and deep neural network of the DQN algorithm without requiring additional networks or parameters. Finally, we have shown that Double DQN finds better policies, obtaining new state-of-the-art results on the Atari 2600 domain.
items.zh: 本文有五个贡献。首先，我们展示了为什么 Q-learning 在大规模问题中可能过度乐观，即使这些问题是确定性的，这是由于学习的固有估计误差。其次，通过分析 Atari 游戏上的价值估计，我们表明这些过高估计在实践中比以前认识到的更常见和更严重。第三，我们展示了 Double Q-learning 可以在大规模上成功减少这种过度乐观，从而产生更稳定和可靠的学习。第四，我们提出了一种称为 Double DQN 的具体实现，它使用 DQN 算法的现有架构和深度神经网络，无需额外的网络或参数。最后，我们展示了 Double DQN 找到了更好的策略，在 Atari 2600 领域获得了新的最先进结果。

items.en: We would like to thank Tom Schaul, Volodymyr Mnih, Marc Bellemare, Thomas Degris, Georg Ostrovski, and Richard Sutton for helpful comments, and everyone at Google DeepMind for a constructive research environment.
items.zh: 我们要感谢 Tom Schaul、Volodymyr Mnih、Marc Bellemare、Thomas Degris、Georg Ostrovski 和 Richard Sutton 的有益评论，以及 Google DeepMind 的所有人提供的建设性研究环境。
